topic.title: Using a Remote Preprocessor with Local File

body.p: You can now configure and run a remote preprocessor (REXX or CLIST-based) on a z Systems® for local COBOL or PL/I files within . This allows local source files to be preprocessed remotely using z Systems® capabilities before being compiled.

section.title: Procedure
section.p: You can open the property group editor from the Property Group Manager or from an edit session. When you open the property group from an edit session, the property group editor opens to the appropriate page for the type of preprocessor being used.

ol.li:
From the Property Group Manager:
Double-click the name of the property group that is associated with the file you want to edit.
Select the tab for the appropriate programming language for the file. Select COBOL or PL/I.
In the category list, click Editor Configurations. The Editor Configurations page opens.

From an edit session, right-click and click Source > Preprocessor Statements > Configure Preprocessor (COBOL and PL/I Editors) or Preprocessor Statements > Configure Preprocessor (z Systems® LPEX Editor).The Editor Configurations page opens. If no property group is associated with the file, the Associate Property Group window opens for you to select an available property group.

Complete the remote preprocessor configuration properties.
Item Description Example
Connection name Specify the z/OS connection where the remote preprocessor will be executed. You can select the required connection from the drop-down list. abc.test.preprocessor.com
REXX or CLIST invoking preprocessor Specify the PDS member name of the preprocessor that contains the REXX or CLIST script on z/OS. Use the format USER.SAMPLE(PREPROC), where USER.SAMPLE is the name of the PDS and PREPROC is the name of the REXX or CLIST script. USER.SAMPLE(PREPROC)
Preprocessor Input location Specify the PDS member where the program will be uploaded on z/OS before preprocessing. USER.SAMPLE.INPUT
Preprocessor output location Specify the fully qualified name of an output file to be generated and downloaded from the remote system to the workstation after a remote preprocessor is started. It must be either a partitioned data set member or a sequential data set. If you specify a partitioned data set name without a member name, such as MY.PDS( ), the member name of the program that you are editing is used. USER.SAMPLE.OUTPUT()
High-level qualifier for preprocessor data Specify a qualifier to be used to allocate a temporary sequential data set. The product writes data to this data set to be read by the REXX script or CLIST on the remote system. The sequential data set is deleted after the REXX script or CLIST runs successfully. If the script fails, the data set is not deleted. USER
Preprocessor parameters Specify any additional parameters required by the REXX or CLIST script. This field is optional. NA
